sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA 247, 248, 249, 250, 251, 252, 253, 254, 255, 256, 257, 258, 259, 260, 263, 327, 328, 329, 330, 331, 332, 333, 334, 335, 336, 337, 338, 339, 340, 341, 342, 343 & 344 of 2017 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection 2. For hearing of main case 12.01.2026 Mr. Anwar Kashif Mumtaz, advocate for the applicant in ITRAs 247 to 260 of 2017 & ITRA 263 of 2017 Mr. Ameer Nausherwan Adil, advocate for applicant in ITRAs 327 to 344 of 2017 Mr. Munawar Ali Memon, advocate for respondent Restoration applications have been filed in ITRAs 327 of 2017 to 344 of 2017. By consent same are allowed and the reference applications are restored. It is jointly stated by the respective learned counsel that it would be in the interest of justice and revenue that the impugned judgments to be set aside and the matters to be remanded back to the learned Assessing Officer for adjudication afresh in accordance with law. Order accordingly. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Office is instructed to place copy of this order in connected matters. Judge Judge Amjad
